guest_role: Co-founder of Base44 (AI vibe-coding platform, acquired by Wix for $80M)
assertiveness_why: Harry directly contests Maor's SaaS displacement thesis, plants his own data on market dynamics and cloud-switching analogies, and challenges across topics without pure deference.
question_style: Prepared multi-sentence questions with substantive follow-ups; directly contests claims ('With the greatest of respects, that's wrong'); plants his own thesis and data points asking guest to defend; mostly lets guest develop ideas with strategic interruptions.
tone: Formal yet matey ('Dude, you're not going to have people...'); enthusiastic and genuinely interested; occasional self-deprecation; high energy and deeply engaged throughout.
depth: Tracks technical specifics: infrastructure layers (databases, Lambda, scheduled tasks, integrations), margin economics and model routing, LLM provider switching mechanics with concrete examples (Claude 4, Sonnet 4.5, GPT-5, Gemini), vertically integrated business models, and competitive dynamics.
harry_quotes: With the greatest of respects, that's wrong.
Do you think small businesses give a shit about that?
Who's the best and who's the worst?
Do the Pro Tools come down and eat your business?
Will Salesforce be bigger or smaller in 10 years time?
learnings: Software is shifting from fixed, feature-bloated products to liquid, customizable solutions that users adapt to their specific needs rather than conform to one-size-fits-all models.
Real moat in AI builders is the vertical infrastructure—databases, user management, integrations, scheduled tasks—building mini-clouds, not just the coding interface itself.
LLM provider switching costs nearly zero now; a superior model can redirect billions in spend overnight, creating unstable dynamics unlike cloud migrations.
Intelligent model routing using cheap open-source models for simple tasks and frontier models for complex work drastically improves margins while improving user speed.
Winners combine AI with structural domain expertise (AI-native law firms, hospitals, banks) creating real moats, not just software that wraps LLM prompts.
